# Notion SDK for Java - LLM Guide

Typed Java SDK for the Notion API with:
- endpoint-oriented client API (`pages`, `blocks`, `users`, `fileUploads`)
- fluent DSL helpers for content creation (`NotionBlocks`, `NotionText`, `NotionProperties`)
- typed exception mapping for all common Notion API error codes
- configurable HTTP pipeline (auth/version/logging/exchange recording/custom transport)

Java 17+ | Default Notion-Version: `2026-03-11` | Default base URL: `https://api.notion.com/v1`

## Install and create client

```java
NotionClient client = NotionClient.forToken("ntn_abc123...");
```

or:

```java
NotionClient client = NotionClient.builder()
    .authToken("ntn_abc123...")
    .build();
```

## Main SDK features

1. Typed endpoint API:
   - `client.pages()`
   - `client.blocks()`
   - `client.users()`
   - `client.fileUploads()`

2. Fluent DSL for writing content:
   - `io.kristaxlab.notion.fluent.NotionBlocks`
   - `io.kristaxlab.notion.fluent.NotionText`
   - `io.kristaxlab.notion.fluent.NotionProperties`
   - `io.kristaxlab.notion.fluent.NotionIcons`

3. Create page parent helpers:
   - `underPage(String pageId)`
   - `underBlock(String blockId)`
   - `underDataSource(String dataSourceId)`
   - `underWorkspace()`

4. Markdown support for pages:
   - `pages().retrieveAsMarkdown(...)`
   - `pages().updateAsMarkdown(...)`

5. Typed error handling (`NotionApiException` subclasses):
   - `ValidationException` (400)
   - `UnauthorizedException` (401)
   - `ForbiddenException` (403)
   - `NotFoundException` (404)
   - `ConflictException` (409)
   - `TooManyRequestsException` (429)
   - `InternalServerException` (500)
   - `BadGatewayException` (502)
   - `ServiceUnavailableException` (503)
   - `GatewayTimeoutException` (504)

## Endpoint examples

### Pages endpoint

```java
Page page = client.pages().create(p -> p
    .underPage("parent-page-id")
    .title("Release notes"));

Page loaded = client.pages().retrieve(page.getId());
client.pages().update(page.getId(), UpdatePageParams.builder().title("Release notes v2").build());
```

### Blocks endpoint (with fluent DSL)

```java
import static io.kristaxlab.notion.fluent.NotionBlocks.*;
import static io.kristaxlab.notion.fluent.NotionText.*;

client.blocks().appendChildren("page-id", c -> c
    .heading2("Highlights")
    .paragraph(plainText("Status: "), green("ON TRACK").bold())
    .todo("Publish release"));
```

### Users endpoint

```java
User me = client.users().me();
UserList users = client.users().list();
User user = client.users().retrieve("user-id");
```

### File uploads endpoint

```java
FileUpload create = client.fileUploads().create(FileUploadCreateParams.singlePart("diagram.png"));
client.fileUploads().upload(
    create.getId(),
    FileUploadSendParams.of(new File("/tmp/diagram.png"), "image/png")
);
```

## Builder options

| Option | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `authToken(String)` | required | Integration/OAuth token |
| `version(String)` | `2026-03-11` | `Notion-Version` header |
| `baseUrl(String)` | `https://api.notion.com/v1` | API base URL override |
| `rawHttpClient(HttpClient)` | OkHttp 30s timeouts | Custom transport |
| `jsonSerializer(JsonSerializer)` | `JacksonSerializer.withDefaults()` | Custom serialization |
| `exchangeLogging(Path)` | disabled | Write request/response JSON to disk |

## Direct HTTP escape hatch

When endpoint wrappers do not cover your use case, use:
- `client.getHttpClient().call(method, apiPath, ...)`
- `io.kristaxlab.notion.http.base.request.ApiPath`
